chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.S-197 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 2. For orders on M.A.No. 459/2025 (E/A). 3. For hearing of main case. ------ 10.07.2025 Mr. Mazhar Ali Mangan, Advocate holds brief for Mr. Atta Hussain A. Chandio, counsel for the Petitioner. Mr. Aftab Ahmed Bhutto, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh Mr. Ali Anwar Kandhro, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh a/w SIP Rajib Ali Soomro, Legal Brahcn on behalf of S.S.P Kamber-Shahadadkot and Inspector Saeed Ahmed Dahar, S.H.O. P.S. Shaheed Fateh Hayat Mekan. ------ Through instant petition, Mst. Shakeela Begum and Muhamad Bux Bhatti have sought legal protection against the harassment at the hands of private respondents. Case of the petitioners is that Petitioner No.1 has contracted second marriage with petitioner No.2, such affidavit of freewill and Nikahnama are available on record as Ex-A and B, pages 19 to 21). Petitioner No.1 submits that after the death of her first husband namely Manzoor Ali, she has contracted second marriage with petitioner No.2, which annoyed her parents, who with connivance of the official respondents are causing harassment to them, therefore, protection may be provided to them. Pursuant to notice, Mr. Ali Bux Mashori, Advocate files vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents No.6, 7 & 11, taken on record. He submits that the neither private respondents have caused harassment to the petitioners nor intend to do so. Learned Assistant Advocate General as well as Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh submit that the official respondents are ready to provide protection to the petitioners as and when required and seek disposal of the petition.

He submits that the neither private respondents have caused harassment to the petitioners nor intend to do so. Learned Assistant Advocate General as well as Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh submit that the official respondents are ready to provide protection to the petitioners as and when required and seek disposal of the petition. When confronted, petitioners present in person, being satisfied, seek disposal of the petition in the above terms. In view of above, instant petition is disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and provide protection to the Petitioners as and when required. Judge Manzoor
